summary:
The effectiveness of topical steroids varies; while crucial for certain eye inflammations from viral causes such as herpetic keratitis and uveitis, evidence does not clearly establish them as definitively topically better than others outside specific indications including eczema management which lacks clear comparative advantage over alternative therapies based solely on current data presented here. Agent1 reasoned that although topical steroids have an important therapeutic use case particularly against inflamed eyes due to viruses causing issues like corneal ulcers ('keratitis') & swelling inside your eyeball behind it called 'uveitis', this doesn't necessarily mean they're best overall when dealing directly only with dry patches or redness typical seen in cases involving irritated skin known medically termed ‘eczematous dermatitis’. Thus concluding option A about general efficacy wasn't fully supported whereas choice B correctly pointed towards one condition where steroid creams do indeed stand out significantly among available options despite lacking absolute supremacy everywhere else within scope examined today. 

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences:
- Topical steroids excel especially during severe eye swellings/viral attacks affecting vision parts like cornea/iris yet don't universally dominate all skincare problems notably excluding robust proof backing broadest claims made regarding common rashes/dry spots found elsewhere e.g., face/body flares up commonly referred clinically under term "dermatitis". 
Therefore, Option B aligns well given its precise fit into recognized strengths without overstating generalized benefits claimed incorrectly implied otherwise potentially misleadingly suggested
